name: Functional Bulk (6x/Week Hybrid)
description: Hybrid program for getting functionally large: 3 heavy gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) for size and strength, 2 kettlebell + conditioning days for work capacity and fun, plus an optional Zone 2 day. Alternates between full gym and apartment gym so you never do the same thing two days in a row. The 12-min run to the gym is your built-in Zone 2.
creator: Custom

activity.name: Stairmaster Zone 2
activity.notes: Steady climb, 120-140bpm. Conversational pace. Great for legs without the impact.

activity.name: Steady State Row
activity.notes: 20-22 strokes/min, 120-140bpm. Focus on leg drive and smooth recovery.

activity.name: Easy Run
activity.notes: Conversational pace, 120-140bpm. Can do this outdoors for a change of scenery.

activity.name: Assault Treadmill Intervals
activity.notes: Not Zone 2 — this is Zone 5 fun. 30s sprint / 30s rest × 8 rounds. Do this if you're feeling fired up.

tips: Gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) are for building size — go heavy, rest 2-3 min between sets
KB days are for conditioning and fun — lighter weight, keep moving, build work capacity
The 12-min run to/from the full gym = 72 min/week of Zone 2 for free
Day 2 (KB Shred) is structured strength; Day 4 (KB Blast) is fast conditioning — different stimulus
Day 6 is truly optional — rest if you're beat, assault tread if you're fired up
Pair the KB Conditioning Blast with 15 min on the assault treadmill or stairmaster for a complete session
For the Functional Bulk goal: eat in a slight surplus, prioritize protein, sleep 7-8 hours
Progress the gym lifts weekly (more weight or reps), progress KB by adding rounds or going heavier
This is meant to be sustainable — if you're consistently exhausted, take Day 6 as rest
Do the Daily Mobility (10 min) every morning — cat-cow, dead bugs, bird dogs, hip openers, plank. Won't affect recovery. Doubles as a warmup on training days.
restGuidelines: Gym days: 2-3 min between working sets. KB days: minimal rest within circuits, 45-60s between rounds. Zone 2: keep HR 120-140bpm.
